title: HK-51 series assassin droid
content:

{{Quote|Manifesto: The maker created me for the sole function of deleting high-ranking organic troublemakers from the galaxy. These deletions must be conspicuous, and provide an object lesson to other organic troublemakers! All other mission parameters are secondary.|HK-51|audio=DidCulatorProgramADroid-TOR.flac|<ref>{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|A Little Rusty}}</ref>}}
The '''HK-51 series assassin droid''' was a model of [[HK-series assassin droid|Hunter-Killer series]] [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] that was manufactured by the [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] shortly before the end of the [[Great Galactic War]].

==History==
{{Quote|Schematics for HK generation 51: Blaster to accuracy ratio: increased by 21%. Durasteel impact frame resistance: increased. Conclusion: halting production. Units are in standby mode, pending further testing of behavior cores and assassination protocols.|Log of the HK-51's development status|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
Despite the ban on [[HK-series assassin droid]]s by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] started to develop and manufacture several HK-51s on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] in secret during the late stages of the [[Great Galactic War]]. This order was for the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]], who intended to deploy the new droids around a decade into the following [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]].<ref name="intel" />

===Lost and found===
[[File:Fatality_interior.png|thumb|right|250px|Multiple HK-51 units stored aboard the ''[[Fatality]]'']]
Sometime after [[3668 BBY]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] warship ''[[Fatality]]'', carrying the droid shipment, was hijacked by a [[Dread Masters' apprentice]], who attempted to land and deploy them upon the planet [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]] to in a bid to rescue his captured masters. The Republic's unit stationed on the planet shot down the vessel, which crashed and sank into the frozen waters of [[Section X]].<ref>{{TORcite|type=codex|The Fatality Crash}}</ref> Not only was the only known shipment of HK-51 units lost, but [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] of unknown affiliation managed to delete all schematics of HK-51's design from Czerka's network. The only known remaining copy rested on board the disappeared Czerka [[research vessel]] called the ''[[Theoretika]]'', which lost connection with Czerka's network, saving it from the slicer's attack.<ref name="intel" /> The ''Fatality'' and its lethal cargo remained hidden until around [[3639 BBY]], when the escaped Dread Masters finally located their stolen cargo.<ref name="Fatality">{{TORcite|type=faction|Imperial|Fatal Errors|Section X}}</ref>

Upon its rediscovery, the [[Dread Masters]] swiftly secured the site. They fought off severe attacks from both Republic and Imperial forces, causing major causalities for both sides. The Dread Masters managed to hold onto the wreck, attempting to access its cargo-hold containing the HK-51s, until an individual of great importance fought their way through the Dread Masters forces and entered the cargo hold, but was disappointed to find that almost all HK-51s aboard were beyond repair after the crash and years of exposure to the frozen environment. Only a single HK-51 was salvageable, but even he needed several critical parts replaced. Unfortunately, due to the loss of the HK-51 schematics, not even Czerka had the parts required. Undeterred, the individual left to locate the wreck of the ''Theoretika'' and the last copy of the lost schematics.<ref name="Fatality" /><ref>{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|The Fatality|Section X}}</ref>

Arriving on the ''Theoretika'' and discerning what had happened to the wreck, the individual defeated the monstrous experiments on board, found and downloaded the HK-51 schematics. After departing the wreck, they began the search for the parts HK-51 required. Their search led them to the [[Outlaw's Den]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where they found a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] who sold them a wrapped HK transistor, as well as a Burba Seismic Excavator/Scanner, which would help locate the remaining parts. The individual searched in the [[Sinking City]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and the [[Glacial Fissure]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], as well as the [[Dark Temple]] on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] and the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The loyalty chip was recovered from the legendary [[The Force/Legends|Force]] user [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] upon the individual's encountering the ancient [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], while the Weapons Protocol software was found in the memory banks of the infamous assassin droid [[HK-47]], who had been found, repaired, and upgraded by the newly proclaimed Emperor [[Darth Malgus|Malgus]]. Returning to Section X, the individual fought their way through the [[Dread Host]] forces and reentered the ''Fatality''. With the help of a droid engineer assigned to them, they repaired and reactivated the sole surviving [[HK-51]]. Upon activation, the droid drew his weapon and frightened off the engineer before cheerfully greeting his new master.<ref name="Fatality" />

===An efficient and loyal companion===
{{Quote|Declaration: Troublemakers beware--the Maker's greatest assassin is coming for you all!.|HK-51|<ref>{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|Troublemakers Beware}}</ref>}}
[[File:HK-51_Revealed.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A close-up of an HK-51 series assassin droid]]
While serving his new master, HK-51 started to become aware that his combat and assassination efficiency was swiftly declining. Analysis of the trend showed that if this continued, the droid would eventually be unable to function. The problem was discovered to be a failsafe subroutine buried deep within HK-51 programming. This failsafe would cause the eventual shutdown unless HK-51 successfully assassinated at least one individual on a implanted kill list, though killing a target would slow the degradation of his protocols.<ref>{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|Cracked Code}}</ref> His master dispatched HK-51 to assassinate one of the targets, a mission that HK-51 successfully completed, and after the mission reported that his protocols were as efficient as the day he was manufactured.<ref>{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|Optimal Performance}}</ref> Coming to admire his master, almost to the level of idolization, HK-51 became worried that one day he would be reprogrammed to turn on the master that he respected. Searching for a way to resolve this, HK-51 found a loyalty subroutine that would permanently set his loyalty to his master and anyone his master specified. His master agreed to activate the subroutine, and HK-51 was overjoyed, congratulating his master and suggesting they celebrate by killing a large number of enemies.<ref>{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|Of Masters and the Maker}}</ref>

Although his master disappeared during the [[Eternal Empire conquest]] in [[3636 BBY]], HK-51 was reunited with them in [[3631 BBY]], when his master was rescued from captivity on [[Zakuul]] and became [[Alliance Commander|the Commander]] of the [[Eternal Alliance|Alliance]] that opposed the [[Eternal Empire]]. HK-51 reunited with his master in the [[Alliance base]] on [[Odessen]], where during a party at the Alliance [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], the droid was confronted by [[Tora]], who mistook him for [[HK-55]], another [[HK-series assassin droid]] droid she recently encountered on the [[Asylum]].<ref name="KOTFE 9" />

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Encouragement: Veiled mockery is an inefficient means of expression. If I did not like you, master, you would be dead.|HK-51|<ref name="PA">{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|Permanent Assignment}}</ref>}}
[[File:HK-51_and_Tora.jpg|thumb|250px|right|The Commander's HK-51 on Odessen.]]
HK-51 series were designed to be lethally efficient assassins and to take great enjoyment and pride in his duty. They were programmed to be very polite, cheerful and friendly to their masters and allies. Their programmers felt that previous models were too unsettling in their brutality, and HK-51 series did not seem to express any sort of hatred or disgust with organic beings or other droids that was common with previous HK models. HK-51 series also expressed dislike of political backstabbing and faction infighting, though they would partake in both if ordered to by their masters.<ref name="Efficiency">{{TORcite|type=conversation|HK-51|All About Efficiency}}</ref> Outwardly, HK-51 series appeared to be nothing more than an antique [[Systech Corporation/Legends|Systech Corporation]] [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]]. This was the norm for most HK-series droids and one of the reasons for their unrivaled lethality.<ref>{{TORcite|type=codex|HK-51}}</ref>

HK-51 series featured several improvements and upgrades over its predecessor models. Besides being more armored and accurate than previous models, HK-51 series were far more mobile and maneuverable, capable of faster and more fluid motions, almost able to replicate those of organic humanoids, perform leaps and perform combat rolls. HK-51 series also had built-in weapons and equipment: wrist mounted micro-[[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launchers]] and [[railgun]], hidden [[Smoke grenade/Legends|smoke grenades]], a [[Stealth field generator/Legends|stealth field generator]], and the ability to repair themselves if critically damaged.<ref name="TOR" /> If all other options failed, HK-51 series was implanted with a powerful explosive which they could activate in a kamikaze attack to complete their mission.<ref name="intel" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Revelation: We have already begun construction on a new generation of HK units. When the last of our deficiencies are accounted for and corrected, they shall be the most formidable assassination droids in the galaxy.|An HK-50 assassination droid|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
[[File:Hk51full.jpg|thumb|150px|HK-51's appearance in [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords|''The Sith Lords'']]]]
In the unfinished part of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', [[HK-47]] would confront [[G0-T0]] on [[Malachor V]], and a group of HK-51 droids would come and help him defeat G0-T0, if HK-47 had programmed them to aid him while in the [[HK Manufacturing Plant]]. HK-47 could also choose to destroy them, causing him to lose to G0-T0. The HK Manufacturing Plant and all related content was cut from the released version of the game and only appear in fan-created mods.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

HK-51 droids lated appeared in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' as companion characters available for all classes. Unlike all other companions, obtaining HK-51 requires the completion of a specific quest chain that begins in the [[Section X]] area of [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]] and leads to the discovery of the crashed Imperial battlecruiser ''[[Fatality]]'', carrying a full complement of damaged HK-51s in which only one proved to be salvageable. To claim HK-51 for themselves, the player must explore the abandoned Czerka Corporation cruiser ''[[Theoretika]]'' in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], and obtain a set of HK schematics. Afterwards, they must collect certain HK parts from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], as well as the [[Emperor's Fortress|False Emperor]] and either the [[Battle of the Maelstrom|Maelstrom Prison]] (Republic) or [[Foundry (space station)|the Foundry]] (Empire) flashpoints. After the parts are collected, the player must return to the ''Fatality'' wreck on Belsavis, and defeat a Sith of the [[Dread Host]] called "The [[Lord of Agony]]," after which the player may claim their HK-51.<ref name="Fatality" />

Players of both factions acquire slightly different version of HK-51, with unique conversations and ambient dialogue, as well the list of three targets, one of whom the player must single out for HK-51 to kill. However, during the ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Fallen Empire|Knights of the Fallen Empire]]'' the characters of the original eight classes disappear and HK-51 appears as [[Alliance Commander|the Commander's]] droid, provided the player unlocked him prior to the expansion.<ref name="KOTFE 9" /> HK-51 provides commentaries on the environmental surroundings only on [[Makeb]] and participates in the cutscene between the player and [[HK-47]] during the "False Emperor" flashpoint, in which HK-51 claims that HK-47 is an inferior model, while HK-47 insists that he is still the pinnacle of HK droid models.<ref>{{TORcite|type=flashpoint|The False Emperor}}</ref>
